other_observers: none; Bill Maynard - I found this bird
specific_location: Near the headquarters of Chico Basin Ranch at the far north end of HQ pond where the road bisects the marsh.
description: Empidonax flycatcher by size, eyering, structure, including bright orange mandible and wings shorter than Contopus pewees.  Large Emip with noticeable small round whitish eyering. Crested head with large broad bill with undersides of mandible orange, although bill appears shorter on one photo due to foreshortenning. Nape and back greenish brown. Bird in shade but through scope bird appearded noticeably greenish. Wings short with medium primary projection. Tail appears longish and dark gray contrasting with back color but the same dark gray as the wings. Buff broad wingbars contrast with dark gray wings. Throat whitish and contrasted with darker light breast and strongly contrast with sides of head. Tibia/tarsus dark gray as were feet.Behaviors: Foraged low in Russian olives with cattail and weedy border. Short flights from one low perch to another. Moves into middle of tree when flushed by me.Call: none heardPlumage: junvenal
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
